name: Brownie Cakes in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
1. Sterilize two 1-pint straight-sided wide-mouth canning jars (specifically made for canning jams and jellies) lids and rings by boiling them in water for 10 minutes. Keep the lids and rings in the hot water until ready to use; set aside.
2. In a small bowl, stir together flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon (if desired).
3. In a medium saucepan, combine butter or margarine, water, and cocoa powder. Heat and stir until butter is melted and mixture is well blended. Remove from heat and stir in the flour mixture.
4. Add buttermilk, egg, and vanilla; beat by hand until smooth. Stir in nuts.
5. Pour mixture into prepared canning jars.
6. Place jars onto a cookie sheet. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
7. Bake for 35-40 minutes, or until a pick inserted deep into each cake comes out clean. Remove cakes from the oven, one at a time.
8. Place a lid on each jar, then a ring. Screw down tightly. Use heavy-duty mitts; the jars will be hot.
9. Place jars onto your counter to cool. You'll hear a "plinking" sound when they seal. If you miss the sound, press on the lids; they shouldn't move at all, indicating they've sealed.
10. Store cakes in a cool, dark place. They should last up to a year.
Recipe by Ellie Collin for FOOD AND WINE CLUB, posted by Joan Mershon on FIDO COOKING.
